channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: SpaceX gains 11% in premarket trading as momentum builds
item.description: CEO Elon Musk said Sunday that the company "might be able to reach approximately" $1 trillion revenue in 2030."

item.title: U.S. stock futures are flat after Dow record close on U.S.-Iran deal; Nikkei 225 hits record high: Live updates
item.description: On Monday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed to new intraday and closing records on a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Qualcomm CEO says AI agents will replace apps — as chip giant works on 40 new AI-powered devices
item.description: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon said he is bullish on smart glasses which could eventually become as big as the smartphone.

item.title: SpaceX IPO raises total of $85.7 billion as underwriters exercise 'greenshoe' overallotment option
item.description: Elon Musk's space and artificial intelligence company raised an initial $75 billion on Thursday.

item.title: This cybersecurity stock is up 80% since April. Joe Terranova says it's still a buy
item.description: CrowdStrike Holdings still has room to run, even after soaring more than 80% since April 10, according to investor Joe Terranova.

item.title: Biotech IPO window is opening but big pharma M&A still sets the pace, top bankers tell CNBC
item.description: Biopharma dealmaking is driven by pressure to top up drug pipelines ahead of major patent expirations later this decade.

item.title: China economy weakens further in May as retail sales post first drop in over three years
item.description: China's retail sales fell for the first time in more than three years in May, while urban investment contracted more than expected, adding to signs the economic slump deepened.

item.title: Bank of Japan hikes rates to 1%, highest since 1995, as yen and inflation worries take hold
item.description: This is the BOJ's first hike since December, when it raised rates to its current level of 0.75%, also its highest level in over 30 years.

item.title: Gold sell-off was a 'reset,' says Barclays — and these mining stocks could benefit from a rebound
item.description: Gold's safe haven status was called into question during the war as the specter of higher interest rates weighed on the non-yielding asset.

item.title: South Korean defense stocks surge on prospects for post-Iran-war sales boosts
item.description: South Korean defense stocks have risen as there will be "numerous export pipelines" for companies to sell to the Middle East.

item.title: Warsh's Fed is likely to hold rates steady — what the leadership change could mean for your money
item.description: The Federal Reserve's June meeting, the first helmed by new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, may impact many consumer borrowing and savings rates down the road.

item.title: Sen. Warren asks Trump if administration plans to raise Social Security retirement age
item.description: In a new letter to President Donald Trump, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., asks what the administration's plans are for Social Security reform.

item.title: Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in its first debt sale since start of AI boom
item.description: Nvidia is set to raise capital in a debt sale for the first time since 2021, when the chipmaker was a fraction of its current size.

item.title: Australia central bank warns hikes are not off the table as it keeps rates steady at 4.35%
item.description: In its statement, the RBA said that inflation was "still too high," which warranted to keep the cash rate unchanged

item.title: Gavin Newsom says Trump ordered DOJ to investigate him and his wife
item.description: California Gov. Newsom called President Donald Trump "simply the most corrupt President in American history."

item.title: Fed's 2026 rate path is underpriced by markets if this PGIM call is right
The Iran deal came just in time as Strategic Petroleum Reserve hits lowest level since 1983
item.description: Oil executives have warned that global inventories are rapidly depleting due to the supply disruption triggered by the Iran war.

item.title: Trump trusts Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. It matters for more than interest rates
item.description: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh is expected to hold interest rates steady this week, but President Donald Trump’s trust gives him room to pursue longer-term changes.

item.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: Waiting for AI to lift the whole market
item.description: China's economy sees a lift from artificial intelligence-related industries, while real estate and domestic demand remain lackluster.

item.title: Vance says 'a lot' of Iran deal details to figure out, but U.S. has 'all the cards'
item.description: Vance on CNBC's "Squawk Box" expressed confidence that America has "all the cards" in subsequent talks with Iran.

item.title: I want people to be millionaires — Jim Cramer's blueprint to make money in any market
item.description: In this exclusive interview, Jim walks through how investors should think about identifying a small group of individual stocks to complement a diversified portfolio

item.title: This no-fee Amex checking account is offering a $300 bonus for a limited time — how to qualify
item.description: Open a new Amex Rewards Checking account by July 30 and receive at least $7,500 in direct deposits within 90 days to earn a $300 bonus.

item.title: People in China are watching the World Cup differently this time
item.description: A 12-hour-plus time difference and various streaming options are influencing how people in China watch the World Cup.

item.title: How the Strait of Hormuz reopening could unfold if the U.S.-Iran deal is implemented
item.description: Tanker traffic through Hormuz could quickly increase but it is unclear whether transits will approach prewar levels, according to Kpler.

item.title: CFTC chair Selig defends decision to approve ‘perps’ in U.S.
item.description: Michael Selig said that incumbents will always fear the future, but it's important to develop the new asset class domestically rather than only offshore.

item.title: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California; 8-member crew presumed dead
item.description: A U.S. Air Force B-52 Stratofortress bomber crashed on Monday shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base.

item.title: Wall Street's fear gauge tumbles as traders bid up SpaceX shares
item.description: The biggest IPO has been digested without a hiccup, and Wall Street's so-called "fear gauge" is back below its long-term average.

item.title: Centene to offer buyouts to some employees as health insurer cuts costs
item.description: The health insurer Centene did not indicate to how many employees were offered buyouts or how much it is aiming to reduce its workforce.

item.title: 'Mag 7' is no more, meet the 'FAB 10': Retail investors buy SpaceX in record numbers for their new portfolio plan
item.description: Single-stock retail buying was $117 million for SpaceX on its first day of trading, amounting to 56% of all retail purchases, Vanda Research found.

item.title: Fox to buy streaming device maker Roku for $22 billion
item.description: Fox, the owner of the news and sports networks, said it has reached a deal to acquire Roku for about $22 billion in enterprise value.
